name: Hawaiian Sweet Bread
recipeIngredient: 1 cup pineapple juice at room temperature
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large bowl, combine 3 cups flour, mashed potato flakes, sugar, salt, ginger or mace, vanilla, and yeast.
2. In a medium saucepan, heat milk, water, and butter until very warm (120 to 130 degrees).
3. Add the warm mixture, pineapple juice, and eggs to the flour mixture. Blend at low speed of the mixer until moistened.
4. Beat for 4 minutes at medium speed.
5. By hand, stir in an additional 3 cups flour to form a stiff dough.
6. On a floured surface, knead in up to 1 cup of flour until smooth and elastic (5 to 8 minutes).
7. Place the dough in a greased bowl and turn to grease the top of the dough. Cover loosely with plastic wrap and a cloth towel.
8. Let rise in a warm place (80 to 85 degrees) until light and doubled in size, about 1 to 1 1/2 hours.
9. Punch down the dough. Divide into 3 parts, forming each into a round ball.
10. Place the balls in 3 (8 or 9-inch) round cake pans that have been well greased.
11. Cover and let rise in a warm place until light and doubled in size, about 1 hour.
12. If desired, brush loaves with glaze made of lightly beaten eggs. Also sprinkle with sugar, if desired.
13. Bake at 375 degrees for 25-35 minutes or until the loaves sound hollow when lightly tapped. Remove loaves from pan immediately.
